content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


January 18, 2002


Nathan Randal
City of Monterey Park
320 W. Newmark Avenue
Monterey Park, CA 91754

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-02-007

Dear Mr. Randal:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") and a follow up to my telephone message of January 10, 2002.

The Commission is authorized only to render advice regarding public officials' obligations under the Political Reform Act. The Act includes Government Code §§ 81000 – 91014. Government Code § 1090, which bars public officials from entering into certain contractual relationships with governmental entities, is not a part of the Act and is implemented by the Attorney General's office. We would suggest you consult with your city attorney or the Attorney General's Office concerning § 1090.

The Act does include conflict-of-interest prohibitions. (§ 87100 et seq.) However, the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only in the context of particular prospective governmental decisions. For these reasons, it is impossible at this point to advise on your conflict-of-interest obligations as a city employee. However, if you continue to be a "public official" by virtue of employment with the city, you will be obligated to disqualify yourself from any decision where it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on your economic interest(s) (including the private

File No. I-02-007
Page No. 2

firm for which you wish to work) if that financial effect is distinguishable from the
financial effect on the public generally. (§ 87103.) We enclose a copy of "Can I Vote?"
which explains the Act's conflict-of-interest rules in greater detail.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [illegible]
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\02-007

Enclosures: Regulation 18329
Can I Vote?

sections.question_synthetic: Does the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply to a city employee's potential private employment relationships, and what are the relevant disclosure/disqualification obligations?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC can only advise on Political Reform Act obligations, not Government Code § 1090 (which the Attorney General implements). The Act's conflict-of-interest rules apply only to specific prospective governmental decisions; if the requester remains a public official, they must disqualify themselves from decisions with reasonably foreseeable material financial effects on their economic interests that differ from effects on the general public.

File number I-02-007 indicates informal advice letter. Some text appears cut off at bottom of second page. Two signatories listed (Luisa Menchaca and John W. Wallace).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply to a city employee's potential private employment relationships, and what are the relevant disclosure/disqualification obligations?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC can only advise on Political Reform Act obligations, not Government Code § 1090 (which the Attorney General implements). The Act's conflict-of-interest rules apply only to specific prospective governmental decisions; if the requester remains a public official, they must disqualify themselves from decisions with reasonably foreseeable material financial effects on their economic interests that differ from effects on the general public.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel responds to Nathan Randal's informal request regarding conflict-of-interest obligations under the Political Reform Act as a city employee. The letter clarifies FPPC's jurisdiction, distinguishes between the Political Reform Act and Government Code § 1090, and explains when disqualification is required under the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions.
